hard.next_step: 6/29 - Catch up call pushed to 7/28 - They are still pending a decision for ERP. 6/22 - Narrowed down potential ERPs to Netsuite and Agriware - Discovery scheduled for 6/25. 6/2 - Discovery Pushed out to 6/25 - They have still not made a decision on ERP. 5/26 - Discovery scheduled for 6/13. 5/12 - Moved the discovery call to 6/13 - Mike mentioned they have narrowed down their ERP search. 5/4 - moving to Discovery call this week. They have completed their ERP eval. 4/7 - Discovery call rescheduled for a month later - still going through ERP evaluation. 3/24 Discovery scheduled for April 7th (currently evaluating ERP; once concluded, they will focus on P2P).
hard.ais_why: AI importance is rated 'Important' and the Merlin product line shows Merlin plus Merlin Intake, but the supporting fields (agentic-platform discussion, Gen AI commentary, AI-RFP rating) are all empty. The Feb 18 discovery call with Mike Bailey (23 minutes) yielded only logistics action items and no buyer-voiced AI signal. With one short qualification call on record, the 'Important' tag is doing the work, which is enough for Curious but not for confidence.

north_star_verdict.headline: Live but parked: Bailey Nurseries has postponed the P2P discovery five times pending its own ERP decision, and no discovery has run in 138 days.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 18 Feb 2026 (138 days old); in Formal Evaluation since 4 May (63 days). Close date moved 30 Jul -> 30 Oct on 29 Jun, now 116 days out. Discovery postponed five times (24 Feb, 7 Apr, 7 May, 25 Jun, now 28 Jul), each tied to the buyer finalizing its ERP (down to NetSuite vs Agriware). Buyer stays responsive (last email 24 Jun) but no discovery, demo, or business case has executed. A key action is stalled on an external ERP dependency, so the deal is Slowing, not merely date-optimistic.
evidence: Next Step 29 Jun: catch-up pushed to 28 Jul, still pending the ERP decision.
Mike Bailey email 24 Jun: 'We still haven't decided... Let's put this off another 30 days.'
Discovery postponed five times since February, each explicitly tied to the buyer's ERP evaluation (down to NetSuite and Agriware per 3 Jun email).
Close date extended 30 Jul -> 30 Oct 2026 on 29 Jun, acknowledging the slip rather than a real acceleration.
Only the 18 Feb intro call has been held (23 minutes); no depth discovery, demo, pricing, or EB conversation has occurred.

metrics.narrative: On 18 Feb Mike Bailey framed the problem as decentralized, fragmented purchasing across locations in Minnesota, Illinois, Georgia, Oregon and Washington, with only he and Matt using a 'crude purchase order system' that 'doesn't function very efficiently' and a purchase-through-invoice flow he called 'pretty disjointed.' He explicitly downplayed maverick spend ('I don't think it's a tremendous amount'), so the value theme is visibility and centralization, not leakage recovery. No number has ever been attached: no savings target, headcount, cycle time, or ROI. Quantifying the cost of the fragmented process is the open move.
sources: Avoma intro call 18 Feb 2026, Mike Bailey
Next Step series Mar-Jun 2026 (no business-case discussion)

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified or engaged. The only people on this deal are Mike Bailey (Logistics Manager) and Matt Toso, both operational purchasers who 'do some of the purchasing' (18 Feb). Mike signaled cost sensitivity himself ('systems like Zycus and Zip, they seem interesting. I don't know if they cost effectively,' 18 Feb) and deferred repeatedly to a consulting firm running the ERP evaluation, implying budget authority sits above him. Salesforce contact roles name no finance or executive sponsor. A $120k source-to-pay purchase needs a budget owner; finding and engaging that person is a priority move.
sources: Avoma intro call 18 Feb 2026, Mike Bailey
OpportunityContactRole: Mike Bailey (Logistics Manager), Matt Toso
Next Step history Mar-Jun 2026 (no budget or EB reference)

decision_criteria.narrative: On 18 Feb Mike Bailey named module interest: eProcurement ('what brought us together today'), sourcing ('there might be some value in that'), and spend analysis, while ruling out contract management ('we don't do a lot of contracts') and staying unsure on eInvoicing. The underlying criteria are centralizing decentralized purchasing, improving visibility, tying paperwork together, and integrating with whatever ERP they select. But no formal RFP, scorecard, or written requirement exists, and Mike gated the whole P2P evaluation behind the ERP choice. Criteria are inferred from one call, not formalized.
sources: Avoma intro call 18 Feb 2026, Mike Bailey

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a strict serial process: choose the new ERP first, then evaluate P2P. Mike Bailey said on 18 Feb they were 'in the middle of selecting a new ERP system' with a consulting firm and full-day vendor meetings, and suggested pausing Zycus until the ERP is settled. The email and Next Step trail from March to June shows the P2P discovery postponed five times, each pinned to ERP progress, with the field narrowed to NetSuite and Agriware by 3 Jun. Zycus is externally paced and in a holding pattern; no P2P approval chain or procurement path has been described.
sources: Avoma intro call 18 Feb 2026, Mike Bailey

paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, procurement, InfoSec, or MSA/SOW discussion has occurred; the deal has not moved past the 18 Feb intro and a string of postponed discovery calls. There is no signature authority, approval requirement, or contract timeline on record. This is expected at Formal Evaluation with discovery not yet held, so it is not a red flag today. Visibility will come from the first real discovery session, where the buyer's procurement and approval process should be mapped.
sources: No Salesforce paper-process field or Avoma evidence addresses contracting on this deal

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is clear and buyer-stated. On 18 Feb Mike Bailey described purchasing as decentralized and fragmented across five states, a crude PO system used only by him and Matt, and a disjointed flow 'from the purchase through the packing slips to the invoices,' with the goal to 'centralize as much of that as possible.' Matt added that contracts sit in Docuware and the experience is 'cumbersome' with weak search, and that internal communication is fragmented across email, Teams and Monday. The pain owner is Mike Bailey; it is a visibility and process-efficiency problem, not financial distress.
sources: Avoma intro call 18 Feb 2026, Mike Bailey and Matt Toso

champion.narrative: There is no champion. Mike Bailey is responsive but passive: he learned of Zycus from a cold email ('otherwise I'd know anything about Zycus'), framed the engagement as due diligence, floated pausing on 18 Feb ('maybe just hit pause until we know what ERP'), and has driven five postponements without introducing a senior stakeholder or advancing the evaluation. He shows no advocacy, no internal selling, and no access to power. Matt Toso is a supporting operational contact. Building an internal champion, or converting Mike by decoupling discovery from the ERP wait, is a foundational move before any commercial step.
sources: Avoma intro call 18 Feb 2026, Mike Bailey
Next Step series Mar-Jun 2026 (single-threaded, serial postponements)

competition.narrative: Zip is the named P2P competitor. On 18 Feb Mike Bailey said he and Matt 'had a conversation with Zip a few weeks ago' and that Zycus 'looked kinda similar maybe' to Zip, confirming Zip is in the consideration set and that Zycus has not yet differentiated. Mike also flagged that the consulting firm on the ERP project could recommend another P2P tool, so a new rival could surface post-ERP. Note the ERP vendors (NetSuite, Agriware, Folio 3) are the buyer's parallel ERP selection, not Zycus competitors. The wedge is Zycus's full source-to-pay suite and Merlin AI intake versus Zip's narrower intake-and-procure scope, which has not yet been activated.
sources: Avoma intro call 18 Feb 2026, Mike Bailey
Living-memory competitor record (Zip, first seen 18 Feb 2026)

deal_movement.summary: The deal is engaged but parked on the buyer's ERP decision. The only genuine progress this quarter is the ERP field narrowing to two vendors; the P2P discovery keeps sliding right, and the close date was extended three months on 29 Jun.

items.change: Mike Bailey pushed the catch-up another 30 days to 28 Jul, still with no ERP decision.
Buyer narrowed the ERP field to NetSuite and Agriware (the two remaining contenders).
Close date extended 30 Jul -> 30 Oct 2026, acknowledging the P2P timing depends on the unresolved ERP choice.

competitive_position.summary: The strongest current threat is inertia, not a rival: Bailey Nurseries has parked the P2P evaluation behind its own ERP selection and could keep managing purchasing manually indefinitely (a no-decision risk). The one named vendor rival is Zip (medium), live in the buyer's mind since the 18 Feb intro where Mike Bailey called Zycus and Zip 'kinda similar' with no differentiation yet done. A second P2P vendor may surface if the buyer's ERP consulting firm recommends one. The ERP contenders (NetSuite, Agriware) are not Zycus competitors.

competitors.name: Zip
competitors.quote: Matt and I had a conversation with Zip a few weeks ago. This one looked kinda similar maybe. So we're just kinda in that exploratory stage right now.
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 18 Feb 2026, Mike Bailey
competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate the full source-to-pay suite (sourcing, spend analysis, eProcurement/eInvoicing) plus Merlin AI intake against Zip's narrower intake-and-procure scope; make the ERP-agnostic integration and one data model the wedge once discovery runs.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus fits the stated needs (centralize fragmented purchasing, add visibility, integrate with the chosen ERP), but the buyer's cost sensitivity and unformalized criteria leave the value case exposed until discovery and pricing happen.

best_practice_check.summary: Win-strategy levers for a live-but-parked early deal single-threaded to two operational buyers.
flags: Decouple discovery from the ERP decision: run it now as ERP-agnostic (Zycus integrates with both NetSuite and Agriware) instead of waiting months.
Multi-thread beyond Mike Bailey and Matt Toso to a finance/budget owner; a $120k source-to-pay decision has no economic buyer engaged.
Differentiate against Zip before the buyer cements the 'kinda similar' parity view: lead with full suite plus Merlin intake on their decentralized-purchasing pain.

critical_signals.text: The last real exchange was Mike Bailey's 24 Jun note pushing the catch-up another 30 days to 28 Jul with the ERP decision still open, so the deal is parked on his timeline.
Zip is the live alternative and Mike called Zycus 'kinda similar' after meeting them on 18 Feb, meaning we have not yet differentiated.
Only two operational purchasers, Mike Bailey and Matt Toso, are engaged and we have never met a budget owner on a $120k decision.
Mike flagged cost sensitivity early ('I don't know if they cost effectively', 18 Feb) and no ROI or value case has been established.

items.name: Mike Bailey
items.title: Logistics Manager
items.risk: Responsive but passive operational contact; deferring the whole evaluation to the ERP decision and a consulting firm, cannot sponsor a $120k purchase alone.

items.name: Matt Toso
items.risk: Supporting purchaser, co-evaluated Zip; no solo engagement or decision authority.
Budget owner for the P2P purchase is unidentified in Salesforce and has never been contacted; likely sits above Mike given the consultant-led ERP process.
items.source: Inference from 18 Feb call and absence of any finance contact role

champion_strength.summary: No champion. Mike Bailey is a responsive but passive coordinator who learned of Zycus from a cold email, called the engagement due diligence, and has driven five postponements without advancing the deal or introducing a senior stakeholder. There is no advocacy, no internal selling, and no access to power. He can be developed if Zycus gives him a reason to act now (ERP-agnostic discovery) rather than wait.
champion_strength.source: Avoma intro call 18 Feb 2026; email/Next Step trail Mar-Jun 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Steve pitched Merlin intake and the AP SmartDesk on 18 Feb and Mike showed a flicker of interest ('you said it'll read an email and turn it into a purchase order?'), but no agentic or AI value story has been carried into a discovery, and the buyer has voiced no AI need. The AI narrative is under-positioned relative to the Merlin/Merlin Intake product scope.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS is 4.0 / Moderate, driven by the 'Important' AI tag and the Merlin plus Merlin Intake product line, with no rep-captured buyer AI commentary; the single 23-minute call surfaced no buyer AI signal, so the field slightly overstates confirmed appetite.

items.detail: P2P evaluation is gated behind the buyer's unresolved ERP selection; discovery has slipped five times and could keep sliding.

items.detail: Do-nothing / delay risk: Bailey Nurseries can keep managing purchasing manually and never sign, a no-decision loss rather than a competitive one.

items.detail: Single-threaded to two operational purchasers; no budget owner or executive sponsor engaged on a $120k decision.
Cost sensitivity voiced early ('I don't know if they cost effectively') with no ROI or value case built.

confidence_signals.summary: Buyer stays reachable by email but the cadence is thinning into serial 30-day postponements with no substantive progress, so engagement is cooling.
items: Five discovery postponements since February, each a 30-day slip.
Last two-way exchange was a 24 Jun email deferring another month.
No depth discovery, demo, or business case has run in 138 days.

scope_change.detail: Product scope unchanged since creation (iSource, ML iAnalyze, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake); no narrowing or expansion evidenced.

customer_preference.evidence: Exploratory and neutral throughout: 'we're just kinda in that exploratory stage'; called Zycus and Zip 'kinda similar'; wanted to pause pending the ERP decision (18 Feb).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only, decentralized purchasing and poor visibility, no savings, ROI, or cycle-time numbers; Mike downplayed maverick spend (18 Feb).

items.expected_effect: Decouples P2P from the ERP wait and recovers months of stalled momentum.
